name: Credential Stuffing Attack
severity: High (especially in financial, SaaS or personal data services)
detection: SIEM, IAM logs, WAF, fraud detection systems, application logs, CDN
scenario: An attacker uses automated tools and botnets to test large volumes of stolen credentials (typically from dark web breaches) against a login portal in hopes of reusing valid username-password combinations. This can lead to unauthorised access to user accounts and potential data theft or fraud.
tools: WAF/CDN (e.g., Cloudflare, Akamai, AWS WAF); IAM logs and systems (e.g., Azure AD, Okta, AWS Cognito); SIEM (e.g., Sentinel, Splunk); Bot detection services (e.g., reCAPTCHA, PerimeterX, Cloudflare Bot Management); Breach monitoring platforms (e.g., SpyCloud, HaveIBeenPwned); Threat intelligence pla

detSteps.title: Detect login spike and high failure rate across many accounts
detSteps.detail: Credential stuffing produces a high volume of failed logins across many different usernames from the same source IP or ASN. Look for multiple accounts failing from the same IP within a short time window, or sequential username patterns indicating list-based attacks.

queries.splunk: index=network sourcetype=auth action=failure | stats count dc(user) as users by src_ip | where count > 100 OR users > 20 | sort -count
queries.kql:
SigninLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(1h)
| where ResultType != 0
| summarize FailCount=count(), UniqueUsers=dcount(UserPrincipalName) by IPAddress
| where FailCount > 100 or UniqueUsers > 20
| order by FailCount desc
queries.qradar: SELECT sourceip, COUNT(*) as failures, COUNT(DISTINCT username) as unique_users FROM events WHERE logsourcetypename(devicetype) ILIKE '%Azure AD%' AND "Result" = 'Failure' GROUP BY sourceip HAVING failures > 100 OR unique_users > 20 ORDER BY failures DESC LAST 1 HOURS
queries.sigma:
title: Credential Stuffing Attack - Authentication Failure Burst
id: 492412c9-2302-5d08-ad88-eefabf468f20
status: experimental
description: Detects burst of authentication failures - indicator of brute force or credential stuffing.
tags:
    - attack.credential-access
    - attack.t1110
logsource:
    product: windows
    service: security
detection:
    selection:
        EventID: 4625
    condition: selection | count() by winlog.event_data.IpAddress > 10
falsepositives:
    - Misconfigured services
date: 2026-07-01
level: medium
queries.velociraptor: SELECT Laddr, Lport, Raddr, Rport, State, Pid, Name, Ctime FROM netstat() WHERE Rport IN (22,53,80,443,8080,8443) AND Name =~ '(?i)(powershell|cmd|wscript|cscript|rundll32|mshta|curl|wget)' ORDER BY Ctime DESC LIMIT 200
queries.carbon_black: netconn_count:[10 TO *] AND NOT (process_name:chrome.exe OR process_name:msedge.exe OR process_name:firefox.exe OR process_name:outlook.exe OR process_name:teams.exe OR process_name:OneDrive.exe)
queries.security_onion:
# Security Onion KQL - Credential Stuffing Attack (step 1)
# Authentication anomalies
# Failed logins - brute force threshold
event.code:4625 AND event.provider:Microsoft-Windows-Security-Auditing
# Kibana Threshold Alert: count >= 10 per source IP in 60s → brute force

# Successful login after failures (credential stuffing hit)
event.code:4624 AND event.provider:Microsoft-Windows-Security-Auditing
  AND winlog.event_data.LogonType:(3 OR 10)

# Kerberos RC4 downgrade (Kerberoasting)
event.code:4769 AND winlog.event_data.TicketEncryptionType:0x17
  AND NOT winlog.event_data.ServiceName:*$

# Zeek auth logs
event.dataset:zeek.kerberos AND zeek.kerberos.success:false

detSteps.title: Identify any successful logins within the spike - these are the critical risk
detSteps.detail: The entire risk of credential stuffing lies in the subset of stolen credentials that are valid. Find any src_ip involved in the spike that also had at least one successful login - those accounts must be treated as compromised.

queries.splunk: index=network sourcetype=auth src_ip IN ([suspect_ips]) | stats count(eval(action="success")) as wins count(eval(action="failure")) as fails by src_ip, user | where wins > 0
queries.kql:
SigninLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(1h)
| where IPAddress in (<suspect_ips>)
| where ResultType == 0
| project TimeGenerated, UserPrincipalName, IPAddress, AppDisplayName, LocationDetails
| order by TimeGenerated desc
queries.qradar: SELECT sourceip, username, QIDNAME(qid) as event, starttime FROM events WHERE sourceip IN (<suspect_ips>) AND logsourcetypename(devicetype) ILIKE '%Azure AD%' AND "Result" = 'Success' ORDER BY starttime DESC LAST 1 HOURS
queries.sigma:
title: Credential Stuffing Attack - Authentication Failure Burst
id: 69d05e5a-9fe3-5e6a-93a0-6c2b5d860f35
status: experimental
description: Detects burst of authentication failures - indicator of brute force or credential stuffing.

    - attack.credential-access

    service: security
detection:
    selection:

    condition: selection | count() by winlog.event_data.IpAddress > 10

date: 2026-07-01
level: medium
queries.velociraptor: SELECT Laddr, Lport, Raddr, Rport, State, Pid, Name, Ctime FROM netstat() WHERE Rport IN (22,53,80,443,8080,8443) AND Name =~ '(?i)(powershell|cmd|wscript|cscript|rundll32|mshta|curl|wget)' ORDER BY Ctime DESC LIMIT 200

detSteps.title: Analyse attacker IP reputation and infrastructure
detSteps.detail: Stuffing operations use residential proxy networks, SOCKS5 botnets, or purchased proxy pools to evade IP-based blocking. Check the attacker ASNs against known proxy/botnet providers. High number of distinct IPs from the same ASN is a tell.

queries.splunk: index=network sourcetype=auth action=failure | stats count by src_ip | sort -count | head 20
queries.kql:
SigninLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(1h)
| where ResultType != 0
| summarize Failures=count() by IPAddress
| order by Failures desc
| take 20
queries.qradar: SELECT sourceip, COUNT(*) as failures FROM events WHERE logsourcetypename(devicetype) ILIKE '%Azure AD%' AND "Result" = 'Failure' GROUP BY sourceip ORDER BY failures DESC LAST 1 HOURS
queries.sigma:
title: Credential Stuffing Attack - Authentication Failure Burst
id: e56dc01c-c1df-5b79-91ed-8ba9ae786e4d
status: experimental
description: Detects burst of authentication failures - indicator of brute force or credential stuffing.
tags:

detection:
    selection:

    condition: selection | count() by winlog.event_data.IpAddress > 10

contSteps.title: Block attacker IPs and apply rate limiting to the auth endpoint
contSteps.detail: Apply firewall and WAF blocks for confirmed attacker IPs. Implement strict rate limiting on the login endpoint (e.g., max 5 attempts per IP per minute). Consider CAPTCHA or device fingerprinting for continued attempts.

queries.kql:
SigninLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(24h)
| where IPAddress in (<attacker_ips>)
| summarize Attempts=count(), Users=dcount(UserPrincipalName) by IPAddress, ResultType
| order by Attempts desc
queries.qradar: SELECT sourceip, COUNT(*) as attempts FROM events WHERE sourceip IN (<attacker_ips>) AND logsourcetypename(devicetype) ILIKE '%Azure AD%' GROUP BY sourceip ORDER BY attempts DESC LAST 24 HOURS

contSteps.title: Force password reset for all accounts with successful logins during the spike
contSteps.detail: Every account that had a successful login during the attack window should have its password forcibly reset immediately. Notify users via out-of-band communication (SMS or phone). If MFA is not enabled on the service, treat this as urgent remediation.

queries.kql:
AuditLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(24h)
| where OperationName in ('Reset user password','Change user password')
| project TimeGenerated, OperationName, InitiatedBy, TargetResources
| order by TimeGenerated desc
queries.qradar: SELECT username, QIDNAME(qid) as event, starttime FROM events WHERE logsourcetypename(devicetype) ILIKE '%Azure AD%' AND QIDNAME(qid) ILIKE '%password reset%' ORDER BY starttime DESC LAST 24 HOURS

eradSteps.title: Remove test accounts or injected data: If attacker created new users or added persistent artefacts

queries.kql:
AuditLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(48h)
| where OperationName in ('Add user','Create user')
| extend Actor = tostring(InitiatedBy.user.userPrincipalName)
| project TimeGenerated, OperationName, Actor, TargetResources
| order by TimeGenerated desc
queries.qradar: SELECT username, QIDNAME(qid) as event, starttime FROM events WHERE logsourcetypename(devicetype) ILIKE '%Azure AD%' AND QIDNAME(qid) ILIKE '%Create user%' ORDER BY starttime DESC LAST 48 HOURS

eradSteps.title: Patch login abuse vectors: Harden login flow, disable username enumeration, limit error messaging

queries.kql:
SigninLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| summarize FailCount=count() by IPAddress, UserAgent
| where FailCount > 50
| order by FailCount desc
queries.qradar: SELECT sourceip, "UserAgent" as agent, COUNT(*) as attempts FROM events WHERE logsourcetypename(devicetype) ILIKE '%Azure AD%' AND "Result" = 'Failure' GROUP BY sourceip, agent HAVING attempts > 50 ORDER BY attempts DESC LAST 7 DAYS

eradSteps.title: Update password policies if weak credentials are in use

queries.kql:
AuditLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(24h)
| where OperationName contains 'password'
| project TimeGenerated, OperationName, InitiatedBy, TargetResources
| order by TimeGenerated desc
queries.qradar: SELECT username, QIDNAME(qid) as event, starttime FROM events WHERE logsourcetypename(devicetype) ILIKE '%Azure AD%' AND QIDNAME(qid) ILIKE '%password%' ORDER BY starttime DESC LAST 24 HOURS

eradSteps.title: Fine-tune alerting thresholds and response automation for credential stuffing attempts

queries.kql:
SigninLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(24h)
| where ResultType != 0
| summarize FailCount=count() by IPAddress, bin(TimeGenerated, 5m)
| where FailCount > 20
| order by TimeGenerated desc
queries.qradar: SELECT sourceip, COUNT(*) as failures, MIN(starttime) as window_start FROM events WHERE logsourcetypename(devicetype) ILIKE '%Azure AD%' AND "Result" = 'Failure' GROUP BY sourceip, FLOOR(UNIX_TIMESTAMP(starttime)/300) HAVING failures > 20 ORDER BY failures DESC LAST 24 HOURS

recSteps.title: Notify users: Alert affected users about forced resets and possible compromise
Monitor for repeated attempts: Continue enhanced monitoring for 24–72 hours

queries.kql:
SigninLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(72h)
| where ResultType != 0
| summarize FailCount=count() by IPAddress, bin(TimeGenerated, 1h)
| order by FailCount desc
queries.qradar: SELECT sourceip, COUNT(*) as failures FROM events WHERE logsourcetypename(devicetype) ILIKE '%Azure AD%' AND "Result" = 'Failure' GROUP BY sourceip ORDER BY failures DESC LAST 3 DAYS

recSteps.title: Re-enable access: Once accounts are secured with MFA and/or new credentials
Review and test controls: Ensure rate limiting, MFA enforcement and logging mechanisms are effective

queries.kql:
SigninLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| summarize Failures=count() by IPAddress, ResultType
| where Failures > 10
| order by Failures desc
queries.qradar: SELECT sourceip, QIDNAME(qid) as event, COUNT(*) as events FROM events WHERE logsourcetypename(devicetype) ILIKE '%Azure AD%' GROUP BY sourceip, event ORDER BY events DESC LAST 7 DAYS

metrics.name: Detection Time
metrics.target: <5 minutes from surge in login attempts

metrics.name: User Impact Mitigation Time
metrics.target: <2 hours for forced resets and notifications

metrics.name: Post-Attack Monitoring Period
metrics.target: Minimum 7–14 days for affected systems or portals
